flashcards.front: What does LIFO stand for in the context of stacks?
flashcards.back: Last In, First Out. It describes the fundamental access order of a stack.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the push() operation?
flashcards.back: To add a new element to the top of the stack.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the pop() operation?
flashcards.back: To remove and return the element currently at the top of the stack.
The error condition that occurs when a pop() operation is attempted on an empty stack.

flashcards.front: Name one key advantage of a linked list implementation of a stack over an array implementation.
flashcards.back: It is dynamic; the stack can grow and shrink in size as needed, avoiding the problem of a fixed capacity.
